title: Private Boat Charter Fishing in Marbella: 2026 Costs & Tips
html:
For the best fishing in Marbella, a private charter from Puerto Banús or Marbella Marina typically costs between €600 and €2,800 for a half-day trip, depending on the boat size and crew. To compare verified options and book direct, browse boat rental Marbella on BoatHire24, a worldwide marketplace for renting boats and listing your own.

Where to set off in Marbella

Marbella offers several excellent departure points for private fishing charters. The most famous is Puerto Banús, a luxurious marina packed with superyachts and charter boats. Marbella Marina, closer to the town centre, is another convenient option with easy parking. For larger vessels, the commercial port of La Bajadilla handles crewed yachts up to 30 metres. Heading east, Cabopino Marina in Artola is a quieter base near the Cabopino dunes. All provide direct access to the rich fishing grounds off the Costa del Sol.

Which boat to rent for fishing

  1. Open fisherman or sports fisher, best for trolling and big-game fishing; accommodates 6, 8 guests; from €900 half-day.
  2. Motor yacht (e.g., Sunseeker 63), comfortable for family groups; sleeps up to 10; includes a flybridge; from €1,800 half-day. For full specifications and availability, see the Sunseeker 63 listing on BoatHire24.
  3. Catamaran, stable platform for bottom fishing; ideal for 12 guests; from €1,200 half-day.
  4. Rib boat, fast and agile for reef fishing; carries up to 6; from €600 half-day.

How much does it cost to rent a private fishing boat in Marbella?

A half-day private charter in Marbella ranges from €600 for a small rib boat to over €2,800 for a crewed luxury yacht with full catering. Prices vary by season, boat size, and included extras. Typical price bands are:

  • Shared group fishing trip: from €150 per person for 4 hours.
  • Small motorboat (Rib or open fisherman) half-day: €600, €900 for the boat.
  • Catamaran full-day charter: €1,500, €2,500 for up to 12 guests.
  • Crewed luxury yacht (e.g., Sunseeker or Azimut) full-day: €2,500, €4,000 with skipper and crew.

Do you need a licence to rent a private fishing boat in Marbella?

If you charter a boat with a licensed skipper, no personal licence is needed; the operator handles the paperwork. For self-drive options, Spanish regulations require an official navigation licence if the engine exceeds 15 horsepower or the trip goes beyond the sheltered bay. The operator will confirm exact requirements for your chosen boat. For private fishing, a basic fishing permit (about €7 per day) is also required and can be arranged by the charter company.

In summer 2026, the best fishing is early morning before the Levante wind picks up around midday; target amberjack and dentex off the Cabo Pino reef.

At a glance

  • Price range: €600, €2,800 for half-day private charter
  • Peak season: June, September
  • Trip duration: half-day (4 hours) or full-day (8 hours)
  • Maximum guests: 6, 12 depending on boat type
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about private boat charter fishing in Marbella

What types of fish can I catch near Marbella?

Common catches include amberjack, dentex, seabream, bonito, and in deeper waters, tuna and swordfish.

Can we keep the fish we catch?

Yes, many charter companies help you bag and ice the catch, and local restaurants in Puerto Banús will often cook it for you.

What is included in a private fishing charter?

Most include a licensed skipper, fishing gear, bait, fuel, and refreshments; food and drinks are sometimes extra on luxury yachts.

Is the boat suitable for children or non-fishing guests?

Yes, many boats have sunbathing areas and snorkelling gear, and skippers can adjust the itinerary to suit families.

Whether you are planning a day of deep-sea angling or a relaxed family outing, BoatHire24 connects you with verified boat owners in Marbella. If you own a boat in the area, you can list your vessel and earn income from private charters. Visit the BoatHire24 website to start.
